title: erodium cicutarium subsp. aethiopicum
intro: Erodium cicutarium subsp. aethiopicum, also known as the red-stemmed storksbill, is a plant species in the geranium family (Geraniaceae) that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily attributed to its leaves, roots, and stems, which contain various bioactive compounds that have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Erodium cicutarium subsp. aethiopicum include its use as an anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant agent, which can help to treat conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and wounds. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be made by steeping the leaves, roots, or stems in hot water or applying them directly to the affected area.
